name: Web Isolation
description: Standard for self-styling, shadow-grade style isolation of a light-DOM subtree — the missing style half of the #854 scoped-component model (whose identity half — same tag → different impl per view — is built in Web Registries). A scoped component must style itself without knowing its parent (forbids .toolbar button[variant] and @scope (.parent)): the fixed contract is self-referential rules ([variant] / :host([variant])) plus token DI (custom properties, source-blind), with per-scope keying delivered by the system (machine-generated, never authored). The goal is in+out isolation (block the cascade leaking in AND your rules leaking out) of a light-DOM subtree without a shadow root, so native <button> a11y + form participation stay intact. Layer 1 is a native-compile proposal framed as tracking csswg-drafts #11002 @scope isolated (the live standards effort that matches the goal exactly) — cited as the seam, not blocked on its (still text-less) spec; @scope alone (Baseline Dec 2025) is out-scoping only and does not block inbound. Two conformant impl layers realize the contract until #11002 ships: L2 a build-transform (unique-class hashing, zero-runtime, SSR-trivial) and L3 a runtime polyfill (constructable adoptedStyleSheets, Baseline Mar 2023) — see #1364. The isolation-strategy dimension (#1365) is a per-deployment Configurator choice (soft/revisitable): S1 unique-class light DOM (native-first floor) vs S2 shadow-per-component (external-CSS immunity, re-forwarding form participation via ElementInternals), flavor-default S1. Ratified in #1349; full Layer-1 proposal + prior-art survey in we:reports/2026-06-20-scoped-component-css-isolation.md and /research/scoped-component-css-isolation/.
